kind: dense data-model + per-element allocation removal for Rev
lever_summary: Rev allocated a fresh vec![0;rank] coords buffer PER OUTPUT ELEMENT + division decode + Vec<Literal> output. Shared rev_gather<T> uses one reused odometer (no per-element alloc, incremental advance), reflects reversed axes, copies src[src_flat]; dense F64/I64/Bool read the typed slice and write new_*_values.
method: same-bench A/B; baseline = stashed generic Rev; normalized by eval/sort_calib_reduce_64k_i64 (ReduceSum, unaffected) — workers within ~5%.

change.lever: Added rev_gather<T> (reused odometer); eval_rev routes dense F64/I64/Bool to new_*_values and the generic path also uses rev_gather over the materialized Literal slice. Added eval/rev_256x256_f64 bench + rev_dense_matches_generic test.

isomorphism.type: BIT-IDENTICAL: rev_gather odometer coords == decoded flat_idx coords, same reflection + src_flat, same output order; dense output value-equal

note: Main cost was the per-element coords allocation; removing it + dense gives 5x. Dense vein now covers reductions/cumulative/argmax/elementwise/convert/broadcast/pad/rev.
